title: Calisthenics B
subtitle: Lower Body & Core
coaching_note: Single-leg work is the priority here — almost every 2025 injury was right-side dominant. Pistol squat progression builds both strength and the imbalance correction simultaneously. L-sit on parallettes is the skill goal for this session. Copenhagen plank added to Core for adductor strength — start bent-knee given the right knee is still being monitored.

exercises.name: Glute bridges
exercises.form_cue: Drive through heels, full hip extension at top, squeeze glutes for 1s. Don't let knees cave.
exercises.why: Activates glutes before squatting — especially important for correcting right-side dominance

exercises.name: Leg swings (front-back)
exercises.form_cue: Hold a wall for balance. Controlled swing — let momentum increase gradually, not all at once.
exercises.why: Hip flexor and hamstring mobility before single-leg work

exercises.name: Hip circles
exercises.form_cue: Big slow circles each direction. Feel the hip joint opening up.
exercises.why: Gets the hip through full range of motion before loaded single-leg movements

exercises.name: Bodyweight squats
exercises.form_cue: Slow down (3s), pause at bottom, drive up. Full depth.
exercises.why: Primes the movement pattern before adding single-leg load

exercises.name: Archer squats
exercises.form_cue: Feet wide. Shift weight to one side, bending that knee while the other leg stays straight. Go as deep as mobility allows. Keep heel of working leg flat.
exercises.why: Pistol squat prerequisite — builds single-leg strength without the full balance demand. Left side gets equal work to the right.

exercises.name: Bulgarian split squats
exercises.form_cue: Rear foot elevated on a chair. Front foot far enough that shin stays vertical at bottom. Lower slowly (2s). Drive through the front heel to stand.
exercises.why: Unilateral quad and glute strength — forces each side to work independently, directly addresses the imbalance

exercises.name: Tuck L-sit holds (parallettes)
exercises.form_cue: Hands on parallettes. Arms locked straight. Lift hips off the ground, bring knees to chest. Hold. Don't let elbows bend.
exercises.why: Foundation of the L-sit skill. Build hold time before extending legs.

exercises.name: One-leg L-sit attempts
exercises.form_cue: From tuck hold, extend one leg straight. Other leg stays tucked. Try to hold. This is the bridge to a full L-sit.
exercises.why: Progression toward full L-sit — attempt when tuck hold is consistent at 10s

exercises.name: Hollow body holds
exercises.form_cue: Lower back pressed flat. Arms overhead, legs straight and low. If back arches, bring legs higher.
exercises.why: Hip flexor and core compression strength — directly supports L-sit and handstand

exercises.name: Reverse crunches
exercises.form_cue: Lying flat. Bring knees to chest, then extend hips up off the floor. Lower slowly. Don't use momentum.
exercises.why: Lower abs and hip flexor strength — the muscles that lift your legs in an L-sit

exercises.name: Copenhagen plank (bent-knee)
exercises.form_cue: Side plank position, top leg's shin resting on a bench/chair with the knee bent. Bottom leg hovers off the floor. Hips stay lifted and square — don't let them sag. Start bent-knee, not straight-leg.
exercises.why: Adductor strength — directly supports the right-side imbalance correction work. Bent-knee variation first since the right knee is still being monitored; watch how it responds before progressing to straight-leg.

exercises.name: Eccentric wrist curls
exercises.form_cue: Use other hand to curl up, then slowly lower with the affected arm over 3-4 seconds. Light weight only.
exercises.why: Evidence-based fix for golfer's elbow — every session, no exceptions

exercises.name: Reverse wrist curls
exercises.form_cue: Palm down. Curl the wrist up, lower slowly. Light weight.
exercises.why: Balances the flexor-dominant rehab with extensor work

exercises.name: Wrist flexor stretch
exercises.form_cue: Arm straight, palm up, gently pull fingers back. Hold, don't bounce.
exercises.why: Lengthens the flexor tendons — completes the rehab trio

exercises.name: Hip flexor stretch
exercises.form_cue: Kneeling lunge. Push hips forward, keep torso tall. Don't arch the lower back.
exercises.why: Opens the hip flexors after heavy single-leg and L-sit work

exercises.name: Pigeon pose
exercises.form_cue: Front shin parallel to the mat if possible. Fold forward to deepen. Breathe and stay.
exercises.why: Deep glute and hip opener — reduces right-side tightness that contributes to the imbalance pattern

progression_notes: Archer squats: increase depth each week. Pistol squat is the next milestone — add it when archers feel easy. L-sit: add 2s to tuck holds each week, attempt one-leg extension when 10s is consistent. Bulgarian split squats: add 2kg dumbbells when 3x8 bodyweight feels below RPE 6. Copenhagen plank: move to straight-leg once bent-knee at 3x20s/side feels solid and the knee has stayed quiet for a couple of sessions.
